structural_claim: Mathematical objects are fundamentally determined by their relational structure and morphisms rather than their intrinsic properties

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Objects in a category are completely determined by their relationships to all other objects, establishing that structure is fundamentally relational rather than intrinsic
Algebraic structures can be completely reconstructed from their representation categories, showing that mathematical objects are determined by how they act rather than what they are
Abelian extensions of number fields are completely classified by ideal class groups, establishing a perfect correspondence between algebraic and arithmetic structures
Primes are equidistributed among conjugacy classes in Galois groups, establishing that the splitting behavior of primes reflects the internal structure of Galois groups
